headline: China's economy loses steam in April as retail sales hit 40-month low
summary: China's economy stumbled in April with consumption, industrial output and investment growth missing expectations.

headline: White House touts deals on soybeans and rare earths after Trump-Xi summit, while China talks up tariff cuts
summary: The meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping last week yielded new pacts, though the sides have provided differing details.

headline: Boeing secures China deal for 200 aircraft
summary: Boeing has confirmed that China has committed to purchasing 200aircraft following a visit to Beijing by Donald Trump, in a dealthat could expand significantly with potential orders for a further750...

headline: US Says China To Buy At Least $17 Billion Worth Agricultural Goods Annually After Trump-Xi Meet
summary: President Trump brokers $17B US-China agricultural deal until 2028. Agreement also includes preventing Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons.

headline: Boeing is still in hot water over Max crash
summary: Seven years after Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302 went down outside Addis Ababa, a federal jury in Chicago is still assigning a dollar value to what Boeing's failures cost one family. On May 13, that number came in at $49.5 million. The verdict is not large enough to threaten Boeing's balance sheet. ...

headline: Todd Graves’ Billion-Dollar Chicken Chain Started With the Worst Grade in Business School
summary: Todd Graves told Masters of Scale host Jeff Berman that his Raising Cane’s business plan earned the worst grade in his partner’s business class, despite being painstakingly detailed. “The professor said, the plan’s exceptional. I mean, I knew details. I knew exactly what our aprons would cost to get washed,” Graves recalled. The strategic flaw, ... headline: Jim Cramer on GE Aerospace: “Buy, GE Is Larry Culp”
